title: AI-powered trading tools
author: chicadepanem
selftext:
Technology and the market are moving at speeds that humans are no longer able to handle. 

A few years ago, intuition and chart analysis were enough to trade but now AI can help us manage the speed at which news moves the markets and decision-making is not biased by emotions. 

I've noticed that some trading platforms already use advanced AI analysis tools. Which ones are you using?

comments.author: gabotM
comments.body: The market is really moving incredibly fast compared to a few years ago, and using these tools without leaving all the responsibility to them is important. I've tried several platforms, and one I really liked was BingX' AI. It's quite intuitive and has helped me a lot with certain decisions.

comments.author: HelloGoodbye2894
comments.body: It's amazing how these platforms did really changed for the benefit of their users. AI powered tools are life changing tbh

comments.author: bacteriapegasus
comments.body:
AI tools are definitely changing how people interact with markets, but in practice most of what is labeled as AI trading right now is more about assistance than full decision making.

The real value tends to be in speeding up information flow, summarizing news, spotting setups faster, and helping with context rather than replacing actual trading judgment. Emotion control is still very human driven, especially when real money and volatility are involved.

I have found it more useful to have AI as part of a broader workflow rather than relying on a single platform’s AI features.

replies.author: WrongTemperature2550
replies.body: Completely agree with you! i just found out that liquid trade launched co-invest, which is the first way to trade directly through chatgpt and claude. so far, my experience with it has been great!

comments.author: flashamazin
comments.body: I've been exploring how platforms are integrating AI beyond just signals and charting. Some are adding tools for market insights, risk awareness, and faster reaction to market movements. Still feels like AI works best as support rather than replacing strategy and discipline entirely.

comments.author: AzlerVzla
comments.body: Personally, I'm already using advanced AI tools for trading; it doesn't do all the work for me, but it certainly makes my analysis easier.

comments.author: PurpleReaction8004
comments.body: Nowadays of your wanting to invest in the stock market, you're going to need some assistance by using AI because it's simpler to use than trying to figure out which stocks to invest in by just doing manual research. One of the better stock screeners to use is Prospero AI. The app shows a list of curated stocks that are on the rise for either long term or short term growth of stocks and ETFs. It's definitely worth a look

comments.author: Opening_Kitchen_5349
comments.body: I’ve been using SuperTrader trading Journal lately, and it’s been helpful for tracking trades and reviewing performance more efficiently. It saves a lot of time compared to manual journaling and makes the overall review process much easier.

comments.author: quant_geek_
comments.body: If you feed the data, AI can work most of the things which is difficult for traders to do manually.

comments.author: Creative-Elephant-37
comments.body:
I think AI is becoming less of an advantage and more of a requirement now. The market moves too fast for purely manual trading sometimes, especially with how quickly narratives spread on crypto Twitter.

Lately I’ve been testing some AI-assisted tools on platforms like BingX and the biggest difference for me is filtering noise faster, not necessarily “predicting” the market. Feels like traders who adapt to AI workflows early will probably have a huge edge over the next few years.

comments.author: The_AI_Trader
comments.body:
Look into SkyAnalyst AI. It’s a platform that houses AI trading agents with multi-agentic flows for major CFD’s. 

Are you looking to trade or build something yourself? 

If you see a similar tool out there please share.

comments.author: thienpro2
comments.body: The emotional detachment is exactly why I started integrating these tools into my workflow. I've been messing around with the smart grid bots on BingX lately, and it really helps execute my setups without the usual second-guessing.

comments.author: Droppingdubs
comments.body:
Here is my stack I use in terminal Claude connected to obsidian connected to trading view MCP.

This allows me to back test strategies inside of trading view and record/diagnose those strategies using obsidian and Claude

comments.author: StevenVinyl
comments.body: Cod3x and nothing else comes close

comments.author: rogupta123
comments.body: Secureputcalls AI

comments.author: kwame1776
comments.body: Gatekeeper. Built it myself and I’m looking beta testers. It’s free if you Bring your own AI keys.

comments.author: ripmetoopieces
comments.body:
I am using Roverium

Wrote a [review](https://ubi.quest/roverium-trading-bot-review)

comments.author: ettubrutusvp
comments.body: Same, I gave up trying to track news manually. Been running moomoo's api into a gpt model I set up, it flags stuff worth looking at before I even open my charts. Beats chasing every call from random twitter traders tbh.

comments.author: HelloEarthSpaceWorld
comments.body: I use [PutHouse.com](https://puthouse.com) for fully automated options trading!

comments.author: misscoffeeaddict2
comments.body: Removing the emotional side is the biggest hurdle, especially with how fast news drops nowadays. I started using signalwhisper a while back for exactly this. It filters through market data and news velocity to give you clear intelligence without the usual social media noise or panic. It has been great for keeping my head straight and validating zones before the market moves too fast to catch.

comments.author: Solid_Definition_935
comments.body: yeah, it's wild how fast things are moving now. i've been checking out some tools too, and ai-stockscout.com has been pretty useful for spotting quick opportunities with all that data.

comments.author: MaintenanceKey8683
comments.body:
edge has def shifted. gut feel and chart reading still matter but the speed at which news hits the tape now is basically  just impossible to keep up with manually.

I'm using Agents on Public -you build automated trading workflows that execute based on rules you set. Pretty solid stuff, I've been running some options flows fills are fast.
